# oms-audit — LLM Reference

Manual full-efficiency audit of the OMS system. Checks persona bloat, over-activation patterns, ctx file staleness, and memory bloat. Context Optimizer always runs post-task in lightweight mode — /oms-audit forces the full audit immediately.

## Triggers — use /oms-audit when

- Context Optimizer lightweight check flagged persistent warnings
- Suspecting persona drift or bloat after heavy usage
- Before a milestone boundary where clean-up is worth doing
- CEO explicitly requests a system health check

The Context Optimizer runs automatically every 10 tasks in full mode — /oms-audit forces it now.

## Invocation

```
/oms-audit             # full audit: all ctx files, personas, last 10 task logs
```

## What it checks

- Persona bloat: any agent persona over ~150 lines without proportional value
- Unused ctx files: ctx files not referenced by any active agent or task in the last 10 runs
- Over-activation patterns: agents activated in Tier 0/1 tasks where a single agent sufficed
- Round efficiency: tasks hitting round cap without convergence (Router classification miss)
- Memory bloat: facts.json entries over threshold, MEMORY.md over 80 lines
- Task log size: logs over 2KB that should be archived

## What it auto-fixes (no CEO approval needed)

- facts.json consolidation if >40 entries
- Task log archiving (moves old logs to .claude/logs/archive/)
- metrics.md task count reset

## What requires CEO approval before action

- Trimming any agent persona
- Removing an unused ctx file
- Archiving a lessons.md section

## Output

3–5 bullet summary to CEO. Full JSON to .claude/logs/tasks/[audit-id].md.
